title: Drag-to-dismiss modal
design_intent: Drag modal down to dismiss — natural gesture.
reference_inspiration: iOS sheets
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. dragControls + onDragEnd with threshold.
2. Velocity affects commit.
solution: <motion.div drag="y" dragConstraints={{top: 0, bottom: 300}} onDragEnd={(_, info) => { if (info.offset.y > 150 || info.velocity.y > 500) onClose(); }}/>
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: List item swipe-to-delete
reference_inspiration: iOS mail
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Pan; reveal up to action width; snap.
solution:
const x = useSharedValue(0);
const pan = Gesture.Pan().onUpdate(e => { x.value = Math.max(-100, Math.min(0, e.translationX)); }).onEnd(e => { x.value = withSpring(x.value < -50 ? -80 : 0); });
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Snap vs settle: matching motion to action
design_intent: Snap for confirmation; settle for ongoing.
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning: The motion should communicate the state change directly, stay under a short perceptual threshold, preserve layout stability, and provide a reduced-motion fallback.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Custom drag handler
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. onDrag updates motion values.
2. onDragEnd snaps.
solution:
const x = useMotionValue(0);
const snapped = useTransform(x, v => Math.round(v / 50) * 50);
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.
